ETFs (Exchange-Traded Funds) are investment funds that trade on stock exchanges like individual stocks. They track an index, commodity, bonds, or basket of assets, giving you instant diversification. Unlike mutual funds, ETFs can be bought and sold throughout trading hours at market prices. What types of ETFs can I trade with KIRA? You can trade over 2,500 ETFs including equity ETFs (S&P 500, NASDAQ), bond ETFs, commodity ETFs (gold, oil), sector-specific ETFs (technology, healthcare), and international ETFs covering emerging and developed markets worldwide. What are the main advantages of trading ETFs? ETFs offer instant diversification, lower expense ratios than mutual funds, tax efficiency, and flexibility to trade anytime during market hours. They provide exposure to entire markets or sectors with a single trade, making portfolio construction simple and cost-effective. Can I use leverage when trading ETFs? Yes, KIRA offers leverage up to 1:200 for ETF trading, allowing you to amplify your market exposure with less capital. This enables larger position sizes and potential returns while maintaining proper risk management through our advanced tools. How do ETF dividends work? Many ETFs pay dividends from the underlying holdings. These are typically distributed quarterly and can be automatically reinvested or paid as cash to your account. You'll receive dividends proportional to your ETF holdings. What's the difference between ETFs and mutual funds? ETFs trade like stocks throughout the day at market prices, while mutual funds price once daily after market close. ETFs typically have lower fees, better tax efficiency, and greater trading flexibility, making them ideal for active portfolio management. What’s the main difference between CFDs and forex? A Contract for Difference (CFD) lets traders speculate on asset prices without owning the asset. With KIRA, you trade CFDs by agreeing to exchange the price difference from the trade’s opening to its closing. CFDs are flexible, with no expiration dates, and allow profit in both rising and falling markets. Forex trading, however, focuses on currency pairs, profiting from exchange rate fluctuations. While forex is driven by global economic factors, CFDs cover a wider range of assets—stocks, indices, commodities, and cryptocurrencies—offering more opportunities for diversification and strategy. Is forex trading only for professional traders? No, forex trading is accessible to everyone, not just professionals. All you need to get started is the enthusiasm to learn and the capital to trade. Forex trading encompasses various markets, each tailored to specific trading needs. The Spot Market, an OTC market, involves currency exchanges at the current rate. At KIRA, you trade Forex CFDs (Contracts for Difference) based on this market, specifying the quantity and price. The Forward Market, also OTC, allows currency exchanges at a future date and set price, though it's less common for retail traders. The Futures Market, traded on exchanges like CME, involves buying and selling standardized forex contracts for future delivery, also less common for retail traders. Lastly, FX Options offer contracts that give traders the right, but not the obligation, to buy or sell currencies at a set price before a specified date, providing more flexibility.
